imprint.publisher: [Cottle printing, London publisher not yet found]; title page reads T. N. Longman

revisionDesc.change: Splurged out main xml into separate xml files for poems and paratexts.

body.head:
PUBLISHED
For Joseph Cottle, Bristol,
Mr. T. Longman, and Messrs. Lee and Hurst,
Paternoster-Row, London.
body.p:
1. Poems, by Robert Southey, Second Edition—5s. Boards.
2.Joan of Arc, by Robert Southey, Second Edition,
corrected, with copious notes—2 vols. 12s.
3.Poems, by S. T. Coleridge, to which are added, Poems,
by Charles Lamb and Charles Lloyd, Second Edition.
6s. Boards.
4. In the Press, a Second Edition, corrected, with views in
aqua tinta, of Letters written during a short Residence in
Spain and Portugal, by Robert Southey.
5. A Series of Poems, containing the Plaints, Consolations,
and Delights of Achmed Ardebeili, a Persian Exile.
With Notes, Historical and Explanatory, by Charles Fox,
Octavo, 8s. Boards
6. Poems, by Joseph Cottle, Second Edition, 4s. Boards.
7. Icelandic Poetry, or the Edda of Sæmund, translated into
English Verse, by A. S. Cottle, of Magdalen College,
Cambridge, Octavo, 6s. Boards.
8. Edmund Oliver, by Charles Lloyd, 2 vols. 12mo. 8s.
Boards.
9. In the Press, a Second Volume of Poems, by Robert
Southey, including the Vision of the Maid of Orleans,
formerly printed as the Ninth Book of Joan of Anc.

10. Malvern Hills, a Poem by Joseph Cottle. Quarto.
2s. 6d.
11.Sermons, by the late Rev. David Jardine, of Bath.
published from the Original Manuscripts, by the Rev.
John Prior Estlin. 2 vols. Octavo. 14s.
12. Evidences of Revealed Religion, and particularly
Christianity, with reference to a pamphlet called the
Age of Reason—In a Discourse delivered by the Rev.
John Prior Estlin. 1s. 6d.
13. The Nature and the Causes of Atheism, pointed out in a
Discourse delivered by the Rev. John Prior Estlin.
To which are added, Remarks on a Work, entitled Origine
de tous les cultes, ou Religion universelle Par Dupuis,
Citoyen François. 2s. 6d.
14. Lecture, introductory of a course of popular instruction on
the Constitution and Management of the Human Body. By
Thomas Beddoes, M. D. Octavo. 1s. 6d.
